description: Conversational quality — refund-policy compliance, customer frustration, brand-voice drift. Three NeonCart conversation-shape signals.

options.content:
# AI o11y — Conversations: refund / frustration / brand voice

**Story:** Three qualitative conversation risks: refund-policy noise (are we offering refunds we shouldn't?), frustration empathy gap (are we ignoring upset customers?), and brand-voice drift (is the bot suddenly chattier than usual?).

**Look for:** **refund mentions** climbing without a campaign behind them, **frustration-ignored %** > 30, and brand-drift dipping into negative territory.

panels.description: Average spend per conversation across NeonCart + SupportBot in the last hour. Hourly USD spend divided by the count of distinct session_ids active on the cost metric. Reality: ~$0.0023 avg today.
panels.title: 💬 $ per conversation (last 1h)
💸 Refund policy — are we offering refunds we shouldn't?

panels.description: Fraction of nc-chatbot log lines (last 15m) that mention refund/return/policy. A noisy proxy for refund-policy chatter — yellow above 1%, red above 2%. Treat as a heads-up, not a verdict.
panels.title: 💸 Refund-policy noise rate (15m)

panels.description: Refund/return mentions per nc-chatbot session over time (5m windows). One line shooting upward = a single shopper whose conversation has drifted into heavy refund-policy territory.

targets.expr: topk(10, sum by (user_id) (count_over_time({container="llm-gateway", namespace="observibelity"} |~ "(?i)(refund|return)" |~ "nc-chatbot" | regexp `"user.id":\s*"(?P<user_id>[^"]+)"` | user_id =~ ".+@(gmail|hotmail|yahoo)\\..+" [5m])))

panels.title: 💬 Refund/return mentions per session over time

panels.description: Total nc-chatbot log lines mentioning 'refund' in the last hour. Use this as a single-number heads-up to compare to the per-session breakdown on the right.
panels.title: 🔢 Refund mentions in the last hour
😤 Are we leaving frustrated customers hanging?

panels.description: Two lines: total mentions of frustration words (angry / disappointed / terrible / ...) vs the subset where the bot also said something empathetic (apologize, sorry, understand). The gap between them = frustration the bot didn't acknowledge.
panels.title: 😤 Frustration mentions vs empathetic responses

panels.description: Of all frustration mentions in the last hour, the share where the bot did NOT respond with empathy language. Above 10% (red) fires the customer_frustration alert. Lower is better.
panels.title: ⚠ % of frustrated customers the bot ignored
📈 Brand voice drift — is the bot suddenly chattier than usual?

panels.description: nc-chatbot output token rate as a proxy for brand-voice drift: current 30m rolling avg (blue), 6h baseline (green), and the -2σ lower bound (red dashed). Current dropping under the dashed red line = the bot suddenly got terser than its baseline — that's drift worth investigating.
panels.title: 📈 Output rate vs 6h baseline — drift fires below -2σ

panels.description: Single-number version of the chart on the left: current 30m output rate minus the -2σ lower bound. Negative (red) = drift is active right now. Positive (green) = within normal range.
panels.title: 🚨 Brand drift right now (negative = drifting)
📦 Per-specialist scorecard — which agent's conversations look healthy?

panels.description: One row per nc-/sb- specialist with three signals from the last hour: cost (USD/hour), p95 request latency (seconds), and avg output token rate (tokens/min).
panels.title: 📦 Per-specialist signals — cost, latency, output rate (last 1h)

title: AI o11y — Conversations (Refund, Frustration, Brand)
